name: Alonso-Gonzalez 2025 De Novo Regulatory Tandem Repeats in ASD (Spanish Cohort + SSC integration)
description: Targeted sequencing of about 85,000 active regulatory regions (cis-regulatory elements from ENCODE) in 200 Spanish autism trios, looking for new tandem repeat mutations -- short DNA sequences that repeat back-to-back and are usually skipped by standard exome and whole-genome pipelines. The Spanish data is integrated with tandem repeat calls from 1,637 Simons Simplex Collection quad families to find regulatory regions hit in both cohorts. The paper points to a region on chromosome 10 (DRD1IP/ECHS1 area) as a top candidate.
whatYouCanDo: Pull tandem repeat call sets generated on regulatory regions in 200 ASD trios. Test whether your gene of interest is downstream of a TR-mutated cCRE in either cohort. Combine with the SSC TR catalog from Mitra et al. 2021.
whatYouCant: Access raw Spanish CRAM/VCF files without an EGA Data Access Committee approval. Detect TR expansions larger than ~150 bp (short-read limitation). Get clinical phenotype detail beyond DSM-IV/V diagnoses.
citation: Alonso-Gonzalez A et al. Large-scale sequencing study of de novo regulatory Tandem Repeats (TRs) identifies new ASD candidate genes integrating gene expression mapping, brain scRNA-seq and organoid models. Research Square preprint, 2025.
sampleSize: 200 Spanish ASD trios + 1,637 SSC quad families (integrative)
accessNotes: Spanish cohort raw sequencing data deposited in EGA under accession EGAS50000001395 (Data Access Committee approval required). SSC TR data available via SFARI Base.
codeSnippet:
# Requires EGA approval. After download:
import pysam
bam = pysam.AlignmentFile('proband.cram', 'rc')
# TR genotyping pipeline: GangSTR + MonSTR (see paper Methods)

name: Cao 2025 Cell-Type-Resolved NRXN1 Isoforms (Adult/Fetal Brain + hiPSC Organoids)
description: A long-read sequencing atlas of NRXN1 splice isoforms across single cell types in human adult prefrontal cortex, fetal brain, and hiPSC-derived cortical organoids. NRXN1 is a synapse gene with hundreds of alternative isoforms; the authors developed a targeted enrichment plus long-read approach to capture full isoform diversity at low expression levels. Includes a cerebellum sample from an autism case and organoids from schizophrenia patients with NRXN1 deletions, with mutant isoforms catalogued.
whatYouCanDo: Look up which NRXN1 isoforms are expressed in specific brain cell types. Compare adult vs. fetal vs. organoid splicing patterns. See how heterozygous NRXN1 deletions disrupt isoform expression in patient samples.
whatYouCant: Generalize beyond NRXN1 -- this is targeted to one gene. Distinguish autism-specific from schizophrenia-specific patterns (small case numbers).
citation: Cao L, Fan Y, Ghorbani S, et al. Cell-type-resolved NRXN1 isoforms across human brain tissues and hiPSC organoids. bioRxiv preprint, November 2025.
sampleSize: Adult PFC + fetal brain + hiPSC organoids; one autism cerebellum case
accessNotes: Preprint posted November 2025. Data deposit accession not yet pulled in this scan -- check the bioRxiv preprint Data Availability statement (PDF link below). [NEEDS VERIFICATION] of GEO/SRA accession.
codeSnippet:
# Long-read isoform analysis after deposit retrieval
# Suggested tools: IsoQuant, FLAIR, or SQANTI3 for isoform classification

name: Brudno 2026 Histone H1 Variants in 16p11.2 ASD (Transcriptomic Profiling)
description: Transcriptomic profiling of linker histone H1.2 and H1.5 across idiopathic ASD and 16p11.2 hemi-deletion carriers. Combines post-mortem brain tissue, patient-derived neural progenitor cells, neurons, and cerebral organoids. Authors report MAZ (within 16p11.2) as a transcription factor that represses H1 genes; MAZ knockdown raises H1 expression, which in turn alters ASD-associated gene expression.
whatYouCanDo: [NEEDS VERIFICATION] Examine H1.2/H1.5 expression in ASD brain, NPCs, neurons, and organoids if the authors deposited raw/processed RNA-seq data. Cross-reference with MEF2C/16p11.2 organoid hypothesis.
whatYouCant: [NEEDS VERIFICATION] Access raw sequencing if not deposited; confirm statistical methods without the full text.
citation: Brudno R, Askayo D, Khair D, et al. Histone H1 Variants Regulate Neurodevelopmental Transcriptional Programs in Autism with 16p11.2 deletion. bioRxiv preprint, April 2026.
sampleSize: [NEEDS VERIFICATION] - sample count not in abstract
accessNotes: Preprint posted April 16, 2026. Egress to bioRxiv blocked in this session - Data Availability statement not inspected. [NEEDS VERIFICATION] of GEO/SRA/Synapse accession.
verificationStatus: UNVERIFIED - abstract-only; needs check of Data Availability section

name: Forti 2025 RBFOX/NOVA Splicing in Pitt-Hopkins (TCF4) Neurons and Organoids
description: RNA-seq and splicing analysis of iPSC-derived neurons and cerebral organoids from patients with Pitt-Hopkins Syndrome (TCF4 haploinsufficiency). Authors report altered expression of RBFOX and NOVA splicing regulators and aberrant splicing of genes involved in neural development and synapse organization, including an isoform shift at the Neurexin-3 (NRXN3) locus toward reduced secreted protein expression.
whatYouCanDo: [NEEDS VERIFICATION] If deposited: examine splicing alterations in monogenic TCF4-haploinsufficient neural tissue. Cross-reference with other TCF4 or Pitt-Hopkins models.
whatYouCant: [NEEDS VERIFICATION] Access raw FASTQ if not deposited; confirm sample count without the full text.
citation: Forti PKK, Depieri LL, Hernandes BB, et al. Abnormal expression of splicing regulators RBFOX and NOVA is associated with aberrant splicing patterns at the Neurexin-3 gene in a monogenic autism spectrum disorder. bioRxiv preprint, October 2025, updated April 2026.
accessNotes: Preprint v2 posted April 2026. Egress to bioRxiv blocked in this session - Data Availability statement not inspected. [NEEDS VERIFICATION] of GEO/SRA accession.
verificationStatus: UNVERIFIED - abstract-only; needs check of Data Availability section

name: Lee 2024 Wac Mouse and Zebrafish Models (DeSanto-Shinawi Syndrome)
description: Mouse and zebrafish Wac loss-of-function models of DeSanto-Shinawi Syndrome (DESSH), a rare disorder caused by pathogenic WAC variants that includes autism, ADHD, behavioral difficulties, seizures, and developmental delay. The paper reports craniofacial and behavioral changes, GABAergic neuron impacts, seizure susceptibility in mouse mutants, sex-specific brain volume changes, and transcriptional profiling of Wac loss-of-function in mice. Preprint v4 posted April 12, 2026.
whatYouCanDo: [NEEDS VERIFICATION] If deposited: examine Wac knockout transcriptomes in mouse brain for GABAergic disruption and seizure-relevant pathways. Compare to other NDD gene KO models.
whatYouCant: [NEEDS VERIFICATION] Access raw data without deposit confirmation; generalize from mouse or zebrafish to human autism.
citation: Lee KH, Stafford AM, Pacheco-Vergara M, et al. Complementary vertebrate Wac models exhibit phenotypes relevant to DeSanto-Shinawi Syndrome. bioRxiv preprint, May 2024, updated April 2026.
accessNotes: Preprint v4 posted April 12, 2026. Egress to bioRxiv blocked in this session - Data Availability statement not inspected. [NEEDS VERIFICATION] of GEO/SRA accession.
verificationStatus: UNVERIFIED - abstract-only; needs check of Data Availability section
